title: Why this chronicle matters now
summary: Geeta Persad's research trajectory becomes newly urgent in an era where climate risks are compounding, aerosol emissions are rapidly evolving, and decision-makers need climate science that speaks directly to the choices they face today.

signals.label: Compound climate risks
signals.text: Persad's 2025 NSF CAREER Award focuses on understanding how aerosol pollutants contribute to compound climate risks — extreme temperature, rainfall, and humidity occurring simultaneously — a critical gap in current climate risk assessments.

signals.label: Near-term climate urgency
signals.text: Her 2023 research argues that rapidly evolving aerosol emissions are a dangerous omission from near-term climate risk assessments. With a 2023 NOAA grant, she's investigating how changing aerosol pollution will influence U.S. climate in the coming decades.

signals.label: Science-policy bridge
signals.text: From her HMEI-STEP fellowship at Princeton to her work with the Union of Concerned Scientists and now at UT Austin, Persad has consistently worked to make climate science actionable for policymakers — asking scientific questions in ways that align with decisions people actually have to make.
